body: TUNGSTEN SMITHING -- pass 20 report. Installed all 3 new forge-family agents as Claude skills: forge-4d-alpha (hardening gate), forge-edge-2 (edge management), forge-indicate (signal formation, from tungsten_smithing.zip) -- plus a tungsten-smithing coordinator skill defining "smith tungsten" as the trigger for a coordinated pass using all 3. Confirmed forge_ledger.json baseline: WARRANT#32/SLS(47)/9.61 was the last shipped (pass 19). Shipped WARRANT#33 -> SLS(48)/9.62: forge-edge-2 r1 (cell identity + Sufficiency + shrinkage-prior wiring) -- LE_TRADE_MIN_N=500 named floor + LE_IsTradeable(), plus empirical-Bayes shrinkage helpers (LE_ShrunkMean/LE_ShrunkMeanDemeaned) pulling thin cells toward the global mean drift, prior strength=30. Purely additive scaffolding, nothing wired into ComputeEntryConviction or any live gate yet -- that is r6, a separate later warrant. Compiled clean: 0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning (sesMap, matches baseline). Identity invariant verified by construction (diff shows only the 2 version-banner lines + the new isolated block; nothing existing modified; new names appear nowhere else in the file). Not attached or run -- this was measurement/scaffolding only. Caught and fixed my own process slip mid-pass (edited SLS(47).mq4 in place before copying to the new filename -- reverted, verified restored, redone correctly). Next up: forge-indicate r1 (relationship scaffolding over the 6 CEUS components), then continuing forge-edge-2 r2, applying forge-4d-alpha hardening-gate discipline throughout. Holding for your word on pace/priority among the three agents.
